q: In a circular motion, the object just moves in a ___.[cite: 1]
In rotational motion, the object rotates about an ___.[cite: 1]
A ___ is a body that does not deform or change shape.[cite: 1]
If a rigid body is in rotational motion, all the particles constituting it undergo circular motion about a ___.[cite: 1]
During these time intervals, its angular velocity is changing therefore it has an ___.[cite: 1]
The equations for constant angular acceleration in rotational motion are ___ to equations for constant acceleration in linear motion.[cite: 1]
All particles of an object rotating around the axis of rotation move in a ___.[cite: 1]
All particles of the body move in a circle lying on a plane that is ___ to the axis.[cite: 1]
The instantaneous ___ of rotational object is the same for all the particles comprising the object.[cite: 1]
Since the direction of linear velocity is tangent to the path, the linear velocity is also called ___.[cite: 1]
The direction of tangential acceleration is always ___ to the circular path.[cite: 1]
The tangential acceleration of a rotating object is a measure of how fast the ___ changes.[cite: 1]
The direction of the angular velocity vector is perpendicular to the plane of rotation, and along the ___.[cite: 1]
The direction of angular velocity is usually specified by the ___.[cite: 1]
When the angular velocity is increasing, the angular acceleration vector points in the ___ direction as the angular velocity.[cite: 1]
An object that moves in a circle at constant speed $v$ is said to experience ___.[cite: 1]
The change in direction of velocity constitutes an ___.[cite: 1]
Thus, an object revolving in a circle is continuously ___, even when the speed remains constant.[cite: 1]
This acceleration is centripetal acceleration or ___ (since it is directed along the radius towards the centre of the circle).[cite: 1]
For uniform circular motion, the centripetal acceleration vector points towards the ___ of the circular path.[cite: 1]
Hence, the centripetal acceleration and linear velocity are ___ to each other at every point in the path.[cite: 1]
In ___, an object is moving in a circular path with a varying speed.[cite: 1]
This angular acceleration gives rise to the ___ which is tangential to the circle.[cite: 1]
Unlike tangential acceleration, centripetal acceleration is present in both uniform and ___ circular motion.[cite: 1]
The resultant acceleration points at an ___ between $\vec{a}_c$ and $\vec{a}_T$.[cite: 1]
